entities.name: Discipline in restraint
entities.context: The ability to stay below one's maximum capacity; more critical for sustainable conditioning than maximum effort discipline

entities.name: Local Optimum trap
entities.context: A referenced framework where working at 100% capacity in the present prevents structural changes needed to operate at higher levels in the future

entities.name: Stress-recovery windows
entities.context: Biological systems require specific stress-recovery windows to adapt; foundational to the restraint principle

entities.name: Survival responses
entities.context: Biological response triggered by pushing to limits early; contrasted with conditioning responses

entities.name: Conditioning responses
entities.context: Desired biological adaptation response that enables long-term growth; requires staying below maximum capacity

entities.name: Intensity caps
entities.context: Practical application: explicit effort limits in training programs to ensure physiological or mental buffer for recovery

relationships.context: Restraint principle is a specific instance of avoiding the Local Optimum trap
Contrasting biological responses; over-exertion triggers survival instead of conditioning
Biological basis for why restraint works; adaptation requires specific recovery windows
Practical implementation of the restraint principle in training and development
